response:
[SOPHIA HAS TWO SCIENCE TESTS, BIOLOGY AND CHEMISTRY, WITH 25 AND 20 QUESTIONS, RESPECTIVELY. SHE HAS 45 MINUTES TO COMPLETE THE BIOLOGY TEST AND 1 HOUR TO COMPLETE THE CHEMISTRY TEST. IF SOPHIA WANTS TO ALLOCATE THE SAME AMOUNT OF TIME FOR EACH QUESTION, HOW MANY MORE SECONDS CAN SHE SPEND ON EACH QUESTION IN THE CHEMISTRY TEST COMPARED TO THE BIOLOGY TEST?]

[CONVERT THE TIME FOR EACH TEST INTO SECONDS. FOR THE BIOLOGY TEST: 45 MINUTES EQUALS 2700 SECONDS. DIVIDE BY 25 QUESTIONS TO GET TIME PER QUESTION. 2700 DIVIDED BY 25 EQUALS 108 SECONDS PER QUESTION. FOR THE CHEMISTRY TEST: 1 HOUR EQUALS 3600 SECONDS. DIVIDE BY 20 QUESTIONS TO GET TIME PER QUESTION. 3600 DIVIDED BY 20 EQUALS 180 SECONDS PER QUESTION. SUBTRACT THE TIME PER QUESTION FOR BIOLOGY FROM CHEMISTRY TO FIND THE DIFFERENCE. 180 MINUS 108 EQUALS 72. SOPHIA CAN SPEND 72 MORE SECONDS ON EACH QUESTION IN THE CHEMISTRY TEST COMPARED TO THE BIOLOGY TEST.]
